summary.heading: Profile
entries: Dr.-Ing. in Computational Mechanics with seven years of research in numerical methods, multi-physics simulation, and fracture mechanics. Hands-on experience developing custom numerical methods and using commercial FEM simulation software, four peer-reviewed journal publications with over 135 citations, and international research collaboration. Complemented by industry experience in production software development, workflow automation, and applied AI integration.

entries.title: Software Engineer
highlights: Architected and shipped a pricing platform for 30,000+ refurbished-electronics SKUs on Refurbed and Back Market, handling both sell-side listings and buyback offers, with a monitoring and override dashboard that cut the operations lead's manual pricing work by 80%.

Designed the PostgreSQL service-layer schema on top of the company's JTL-Wawi ERP and built a 24/7 priority-based asynchronous pipeline that reprices the catalogue under explicit margin and grade-tier rules.

Integrated Refurbed and Back Market REST APIs and the Anthropic Claude API for LLM-supported grade and pricing decisions, with automated SKU onboarding and product-image processing for the company storefront.

entries.title: Professional Development
highlights: Structured self-study in machine learning, deep learning, and data science.

entries.title: Research Assistant
highlights: Developed a novel polygon-based SBFEM formulation for transient thermoelastic fracture in functionally graded materials, implemented in object-oriented MATLAB and validated against commercial FEM software.
Published four peer-reviewed journal papers with over 135 citations in top computational- and fracture-mechanics journals; peer reviewer for an international journal.
Designed and delivered a master's-level Structural Dynamics course in ANSYS on a university e-learning grant, including a custom interactive simulation lab for hands-on structural-dynamics teaching.
Taught a master's-level Computer Languages for Engineers course in object-oriented Fortran and C++ with numerical-methods term projects.
Supervised three master's and one bachelor's thesis in computational mechanics and CFD.

entries.title: Visiting Researcher
highlights: Australia-Germany research cooperation (Federation University, DAAD) on polygon SBFEM for coupled thermoelastic fracture in functionally graded materials.

entries.title: Student Research Assistant
highlights: Implemented a polygon-based SBFEM in Fortran and integrated it into the open-source FE code FEAPpv.

entries.title: Seasonal Worker (Assembly Line)
highlights: Spot welding with jigs and automotive assembly-line work.

entries.title: Seasonal Worker (Assembly Line)
highlights: Production tasks on automotive assembly line.

entries.title: Seasonal Worker (Logistics)
entries.org: Amazon Deutschland Services GmbH, Rheinberg, Germany

entries.title: Internship
entries.org: Karachi Tools, Dies & Moulds Center, Karachi, Pakistan
highlights: Practical training in CAD/CAM and nitriding heat treatment.

entries.title: sahab.pk — AI-Powered Ledger SaaS
highlights: Multi-tenant SaaS for retailers in Pakistan: shopkeepers chat in Urdu or English; an LLM extracts structured events into a double-entry ledger with row-level tenant isolation.

entries.title: Free-Body Diagram Toolkit — Teaching Aid
highlights: MATLAB code used in own Ph.D. thesis and in graduate Structural Dynamics tutorials, assignments, and exams at the University of Duisburg-Essen.

entries.title: Formula Student Race Car — Carbon-Fiber Monocoque
entries.org: Undergraduate team project — NED University, Karachi
highlights: Designed to Formula Student Spain competition specification: carbon-fiber monocoque in Siemens NX with CFRP/honeycomb sandwich construction, panel strength testing passed; prior ANSYS structural analysis on the predecessor space-frame chassis.

entries.title: Ph.D. (Dr.-Ing.) Computational Mechanics
highlights: Thesis: Development of scaled boundary polygon elements for coupled thermoelastic fracture modeling.

entries.title: M.Sc. Computational Mechanics
highlights: Thesis: Development of polygon elements based on the scaled boundary finite element method.

entries.title: B.E. Automotive Engineering
entries.org: NED University of Engineering & Technology, Karachi, Pakistan
highlights: Thesis: Design and analysis of motorcycle oil pump.

publications.heading: Publications
